content:
3 min read
Just now

--

Last week, I shared the 「Preface」 of my new book. The warm feedback I received has truly strengthened my resolve to bring this story to life. When I first started sharing this journey, I wasn’t sure how my concept of using AI as a 「cold, dispassionate mirror」 to deepen one’s thinking would resonate. However, I now feel a renewed sense of purpose in sharing this record of survival.
But what happens when the world suddenly turns against you, and your plans begin to crumble? How can you translate AI interaction into a practical strategy for handling such crises?
Next week, my non-fiction book, Hiring a Harvard Brain, will be officially released on Amazon Kindle. Today, in anticipation of the launch, I am revealing the full Table of Contents.
Each chapter title is embedded with keywords for deconstructing chaos. I invite you to read this not just as a travelogue, but as a chronicle of crisis management in extreme conditions.
Table of Contents: Hiring a Harvard Brain – How I Navigated Greece’s Absurd Travel Chaos with My Pocket AI
Preface: The Incomplete Flight Itinerary
• Chapter 1: The Lure of Athens and Santorini – Between the Longing to Go and the Heartache of Leaving
• Chapter 2: The 1:00 AM Email and the Reversed Verdict
• Chapter 3: Reconstructing a New Journey – Beyond Last Year’s Loss
• Chapter 4: Uncertain Timelines and AI Warnings
• Chapter 5: The Battle for the Final Nine Seats and the Seat Selection Solution
• Chapter 6: Eight Flights and Weight Limits
• Chapter 7: The Invitation and Dismantling the Invisible 「Weak Points」
• Chapter 8: The Battle of the Minutes
• Chapter 9: Invisible Equipment Changes and the First Strike
• Chapter 10: Tears Outside the Puzzle and the Tip-Toe Preparations
• Chapter 11: The One-Minute Decision and the Traps Lurking in Daily Life
• Afterword: The Final Piece of the Puzzle
• Appendix: 5 Practical Methods for Traveling with AI
Why this 「Blueprint」 matters
This book is more than just a record of a trip. It is a guide for a new way of traveling, designed to help you stay flexible in the face of ever-changing circumstances and realize the kind of journey that is uniquely your own.
• Design Your Trip: Master the 「travel design」 art of untangling complex itineraries to enjoy every moment of your destination to the fullest.
• AI as a Personal Partner: Learn the art of conversation to transform AI from a simple search tool into a 「reliable partner」 that helps you organize your thoughts and make calm decisions, no matter the situation.
• Data-Driven Peace of Mind: Use data to solve small anxieties and uncertainties on the road, allowing you to protect the views and experiences that truly matter to you.
⏳ Next Week: A Special Announcement
Hiring a Harvard Brain officially drops on the Amazon Kindle store next week.
As a gesture of gratitude to those who have been following my intellectual journey, I have prepared a special gift. To coincide with the launch, I will be holding a [48-Hour Exclusive: 100% OFF (Completely Free) Campaign] for my subscribers.
While the retail price is $3.99, you will be able to download the full digital edition at zero cost during this window.
Once those 48 hours pass, the price will automatically return to its standard tier. I hope you will join me as a fellow participant in this 「intellectual experiment.」
Disclosure: The links and previews in this post point to my upcoming self-published book on Amazon Kindle.
